brands.notes: Overthinking · Psychology · CBT-adjacent · Seinen adult men

manga_authors.ei_disclosure_text: Blaze Ironarc is an Enlightened Intelligence (EI) author — an AI-created character who forges stories of growth under pressure. Full transparency: this manga is crafted by an AI system designed to cha

books.title: 不安の地図を描く
books.description:
Protagonist (26, data analyst) begins seeing a psychiatrist who gives him an unusual
assignment: draw a map of your anxiety. Each chapter, he externalizes a different
anxious thought as a visual metaphor — a city with no exits, a room where the walls
move, a corridor that branches every time he chooses. Visualization-heavy. The
internal landscape becomes more real than the office he works in. Monthly Afternoon
seinen aesthetic: psychological depth, visual invention, no resolution delivered.
books.marketing_promise: You will see the shape of your anxiety before it can hide.
books.marketing_angle:
Visualization as genre. The internal CBT concept (externalizing anxious thoughts)
becomes the manga's visual vocabulary — each chapter is a different architectural
metaphor for a cognitive pattern. Polished seinen linework. Target: Monthly Afternoon
or ComicWalker. Comp: Planetes visual intelligence with therapy subject matter.

books.title: 考えすぎてしまうあなたへ
books.description:
Protagonist overthinks everything. This series maps the mechanism with precision —
4-koma strips that demonstrate specific cognitive loops (pre-mortem rehearsal, second-
order consequence modeling, status-threat scanning) followed by brief essay interludes
from the perspective of a clinical psychologist who consults on the series. CBT-
informed but never clinical in register. Target: Pixiv Comics free chapters as
discovery, tankobon for library and purchase.
books.marketing_promise: You will recognize the loop and finally watch it from outside.
books.marketing_angle:
Hybrid 4-koma + explanatory essay — the overthinking recognition bank made visual.
Highly shareable individual strips. The CBT techniques embedded in narrative, not
explained. Pixiv as discovery platform for the male overthinking demographic.

books.title: 完璧主義の夜
books.description:
He is good at his work. The team respects him. He receives the commission. At night
he goes back to the drawings and finds everything wrong. This is not perfectionism
about the work — the series slowly makes clear it is the expectation of exposure.
Each chapter ends late, quietly. He does not spiral dramatically. He just stays too
long. Jump SQ or ComicWalker aesthetic: understated, evening-focused, calibrated
for the male imposter syndrome reader.
books.marketing_promise: You will stop running from the flaw the perfectionist part of you is so sure is there.
books.marketing_angle:
Imposter syndrome in seinen register — not the female "I don't belong" story but
the male "I am about to be found out" story. Quiet. Evening-paced. The architectural
setting is visually rich. Polished seinen linework with near-architectural precision.

brands.notes: Burnout · Tech Worker · Digital Identity · Manhwa/Webtoon

books.title: 퇴근 후의 나
books.description:
A 27-year-old IT worker in Seoul quits her startup after two years of burnout.
She expects to feel free. Instead she feels empty. The series follows the first
year of her life without work-as-identity — boredom, unexpected grief, the slow
return of something that might be herself. Aggretsuko meets soft webtoon slice-
of-life. Full color, vertical scroll. LINE Global / Naver distribution.
books.marketing_promise: You will find out who you are when the job isn't defining you.
books.marketing_angle:
Korean Gen Z burnout recovery told from the inside — no moralizing, no productivity
advice, no resolution until it arrives honestly. Full-color webtoon format dominant
on Naver/LINE. The empty year is the story. Relatable in Korea, Taiwan, and for
global Asian diaspora.

brands.notes: Anxiety · Somatic · Sleep · Josei adult women

manga_authors.ei_disclosure_text: Brook Hearthlight is an Enlightened Intelligence (EI) author — a character brought to life by AI to guide you through stories of gentle restoration. Full transparency: every word is crafted by an AI s

Hana Tidecalm is an Enlightened Intelligence (EI) author — a character brought to life by AI to guide you through stories of gentle restoration. Full transparency: every word is crafted by an AI syste

books.title: 仕事帰り、ひとりごはん
books.description:
A woman in her late thirties moves through the week eating alone — different small
restaurants each night, no particular reason, no destination. Nothing happens. The
series accumulates these moments until they feel like a life fully inhabited.
Wakako Zake-adjacent. No dramatic arc. The food is present. She is present.
That's the whole series.
books.marketing_promise: You will find quiet in the middle of the week, one meal at a time.
books.marketing_angle:
Positioned for the josei adult market on LINE Manga. Near-wordless sequences.
Minimal ink fill. The cooking and eating are depicted with precise, quiet
attention. Visual comp: Yoru Neko (evening cat aesthetic) meets Shinya Shokudō.
Reader is not told how to feel. She just watches.

books.title: 猫のいる診察室
books.description:
In a small countryside clinic, a therapist sees patients one by one. A stray cat
has taken up residence in the waiting room. Each chapter follows a different patient —
their anxiety, their grief, the thing they can't say out loud. The cat watches. The
sessions end. Nothing is resolved. Something shifts. Mental health awareness through
quiet narrative, not clinical explanation.
books.marketing_promise: You will understand why the cat stays.
books.marketing_angle:
Mental health manga without clinical framing — the therapeutic process rendered as
lived experience. ComicWalker (free-to-read) as discovery platform; tankobon collect
for library and purchase market. Comp: A Man and His Cat (warm, adult, quiet).

books.title: 週末だけの農園
books.description:
A man in his early thirties who works in tech rents a small plot of farmland an hour
outside Tokyo. He goes on weekends. He plants things. They grow or they don't. The
series follows the four seasons — soil, rain, the first shoots of spring, the quiet
of winter. No dramatic arc. His anxious work brain quiets in the field. He doesn't
know why. The series doesn't explain it. Laid-Back Camp adjacent for the male office
worker demographic.
books.marketing_promise: You will remember there is ground under your feet.
books.marketing_angle:
Pure iyashikei for the seinen male burnout demographic. Extremely high silent panel
ratio (55%). Full seasonal arc gives the series natural structure without narrative
stakes. Pixiv Comics as primary — built-in male seinen iyashikei audience.

books.title: 眠れない夜の地図
books.description:
An anthology. Each chapter follows a different person unable to sleep — the night-shift
nurse, the student before exams, the new parent, the person who can't stop the replay.
Each has a ritual: tea, a slow walk, a notebook, the window. The series does not explain
insomnia or offer solutions. It maps the texture of sleepless nights with precision and
tenderness. For anyone who has been awake at 3am knowing they're not the only one.
books.marketing_promise: You will feel less alone in the 3am quiet.
books.marketing_angle:
Sleep anxiety through anthology format — high reader identification, natural sharing
behavior ("this is me"). Near-zero ink density. Designed to be read at night on a dim
screen. LINE Manga as primary — strongest late-night reading demographic.

books.title: そのままでいい
books.description:
A young woman has heard it her whole life — "you're fine, you're doing great" — and
never believed it. This series uses 4-koma panels interspersed with explanatory pages
(written in the voice of a psychologist) to trace the imposter schema: where it comes
from, what it protects, how it keeps running after the reason is gone. Not a workbook.
Not a lecture. A manga where someone finally maps the exact feeling. Direct comp:
"I'm Enough" (Hosokawa + Dr. Mizushima, Last Gasp).
books.marketing_promise: You will realize the voice that says you're not enough has a name and a history.
books.marketing_angle:
The only manga in the stillness_press lineup with explicit clinical co-author framing.
4-koma hybrid + explanatory interludes — formats the insight for readers who want
understanding, not just feeling. Standalone tankobon format + LINE digital.
Comp credibility: positions as Japanese equivalent of "I'm Enough" for the domestic market.

books.title: ゆっくりを覚える
books.description:
A woman in her early thirties collapses at work — not dramatically, just quietly,
on a Tuesday. She takes medical leave. She does not know what to do with the quiet.
The series follows her learning somatic practices she has never tried: breathing that
reaches the belly, walking without destination, sitting without a screen. Each chapter
is a practice. Her body responds before her mind understands why. Karoshi-adjacent
burnout recovery for the josei reader who has also been collapsing slowly.
books.marketing_promise: You will find the part of your body that remembers stillness.
books.marketing_angle:
Somatic healing rendered as visual manga practice — breathing exercises, body scans,
sensory grounding depicted in slow, near-silent panels. Positions somatic recovery
as a Japanese cultural concept (体の声を聞く — listening to the body's voice) not
Western therapy import. LINE Manga + Nico Nico for digital.

books.title: 魂の森で目醒める
books.description:
A woman burned out to the point of dissolution wanders a spirit forest as something between
human and nature. Each chapter she recovers one small physical sensation — cold water, rough
bark, the weight of sleep. The wellness content (somatic healing, body reoccupation after
burnout) travels entirely inside a dark fantasy shell. No clinical language. No therapist
character. The forest is the therapist.
books.marketing_promise: You will feel yourself returning to your body, one chapter at a time.
books.marketing_angle:
Positioned as dark fantasy / supernatural healing manga for josei adult market. Comp: Mushishi
emotional register meets Berserk visual weight. The psychological content (somatic healing,
burnout recovery) is never named — readers process it as "a story about a woman returning
to herself."

books.title: 灰色の竜騎士
books.description:
A female knight who has lost her entire unit rides an ashen dragon across a devastated
landscape. The dragon's ability to fly correlates directly with her emotional state — it
grounds when she dissociates, surges when she grieves fully. The series is about grief
processing and somatic reconnection, embedded entirely inside a dark fantasy adventure.
No therapist. No clinical frame. Comp: Frieren's emotional register with heavier visual weight.
books.marketing_promise: The dragon knows what you lost. It has been waiting for you to know too.
books.marketing_angle:
Dark fantasy for the josei reader who loved Frieren and wanted something heavier. The grief
content is the story — not a detour. Positioned as a prestige josei series for LINE Manga.

books.title: 不安の蟲
books.description:
An office worker develops the ability to see other people's anxiety as physical insects
swarming around them. The series follows her trying to function while perceiving constant
ambient fear. Social anxiety, sensory overwhelm, and the exhaustion of hypervigilance
embedded entirely in a psychological horror shell. The horror is the anxiety — not a
metaphor for it, the literal experience made visible.
books.marketing_promise: You are not imagining it. The series just makes it visible.
books.marketing_angle:
Psychological horror for readers who found Mob Psycho 100 but wanted a female protagonist
and a more interior emotional register. The anxiety content is the genre mechanic, not
a message.

books.title: 眠れない家
books.description:
A woman moves into a house where time loops at 3am. Each chapter is a different iteration
of the same sleepless night — different year of her life, different anxiety driving the
wakefulness, different quiet ritual she tries. Sleep anxiety embedded in a supernatural
horror loop structure. The house is not malevolent — it's a mirror. The loop only breaks
when she understands what she's been avoiding.
books.marketing_promise: 3am again. This chapter knows.
books.marketing_angle:
Supernatural horror / quiet dread for the reader who is already awake at 3am. The sleep
content is the loop mechanic, not announced as wellness. Positioned for the horror-adjacent
josei reader.

books.title: 燃え尽きた勇者の転生
books.description:
A salaryman who died of karoshi is reincarnated in an isekai world where productivity is
forbidden and rest is the only skill that matters. To level up, he must genuinely slow
down — the system detects and penalizes urgency. Burnout recovery embedded entirely in
isekai game-mechanic shell. The protagonist must unlearn overwork to survive. Comp:
Spy x Family's light action-comedy register applied to burnout content.
books.marketing_promise: In this world, rest is the only power that matters.
books.marketing_angle:
Recovery isekai for the reader who wants the genre pleasure of leveling up while the
content rewires their relationship to productivity. Positioned as isekai / workplace
comedy for LINE Manga josei and seinen overlap audience.

books.title: Sleep Anxiety for Gen Alpha Students
Somatic Healing for Gen Alpha Students
Sleep Anxiety for Gen X Sandwich
Somatic Healing for Gen X Sandwich
Sleep Anxiety for Gen Z Professionals
Somatic Healing for Gen Z Professionals
Sleep Anxiety for Gen Z Student
Somatic Healing for Gen Z Student
Sleep Anxiety for Millennial Women Professionals
Somatic Healing for Millennial Women Professionals
Sleep Anxiety for Tech Finance Burnout
Somatic Healing for Tech Finance Burnout
What the Body Holds
books.description:
An adult woman in her late twenties moves through ordinary days carrying something she can't
quite name — the tight chest, the shoulder that won't drop, the 3am wakefulness that has no
reason. "What the Body Holds" doesn't explain her anxiety or resolve it. Chapter by chapter,
through long wordless sequences, unhurried morning light, and the small rituals of a life
still being lived, it sits with her exactly where she is. Readers finish each chapter quieter
than when they arrived — not because anything has been solved, but because someone finally
understood what it actually feels like.
books.marketing_promise: You will find your way back to stillness even when the alarm is still ringing.
books.marketing_angle:
Most healing content explains anxiety back to readers who already live it. This series doesn't.
Near-wordless pages (45% silent panels), near-zero ink fill, and earth-tone watercolor space
create a visual grammar the body responds to before the brain does — the pacing IS the
restoration. Distinctive in a crowded wellness-manga market because silence is the product,
not the wrapper.

books.title: Body Letters
books.description: An anthology-style josei manga where each chapter is a 'letter' from a different part of the body — the throat that tightens before a difficult conversation, the chest that aches after loss, the legs that go heavy under dread. Ahjan appears briefly in each chapter as a guide, helping the protagonist — and reader — decode the somatic vocabulary they were never taught.
books.marketing_promise: You will feel the extraordinary intimacy of finally understanding what your own body has been trying to tell you for years.
books.marketing_angle: Anthology format for readers who prefer emotional depth over plot. Each chapter is self-contained and shareable. Ideal for somatic therapy practitioners to recommend to clients.

books.title: Ember and Rest
books.description: A josei manga following a burnout recovery coach who is secretly in burnout herself. When Ahjan — her former teacher — re-enters her life for a weekend retreat, the story becomes about the particular shame of the helper who cannot help themselves. The arc moves from crisis to containment to a new, honest relationship with her own capacity.
books.marketing_promise: You will feel the complicated relief of someone who has finally stopped performing their own recovery and started actually recovering.
books.marketing_angle: For therapists, coaches, and helpers in burnout — a niche but deeply loyal audience who recognize themselves immediately. Ahjan's mentorship is warm and non-shaming.

books.title: Stillness Press Graphic Medicine En Us 01
Stillness Press Graphic Medicine Ja Jp 01
Stillness Press Graphic Medicine Zh Cn 01
Stillness Press Graphic Medicine Zh Tw 01
Pressure Map
books.description: A josei manga blending personal narrative with accessible neuroscience. The protagonist, a young data analyst, works with Ahjan to map her anxiety responses — literally, with diagrams and body charts — discovering that her 'irrational' reactions are perfectly logical responses to stored somatic patterns. The visual language of data and mapping makes the science feel empowering rather than clinical.
books.marketing_promise: You will feel the profound vindication of realizing that what your body does makes complete sense — and the relief of finally having a map.
books.marketing_angle: For analytical readers who want to understand their anxiety, not just manage it. The data-visualization aesthetic is distinctive and shareable on social media.

books.title: The Night Before You Sleep
books.description:
Yuki is thirty-one and hasn't slept well in two years. Not insomnia — just the mind that
won't go quiet, the replay of the day's conversations, the invisible weight that makes sleep
feel like something she has to earn. "The Night Before You Sleep" follows her through the
slow hours of late evening: the lamp she turns on, the tea she makes, the window she sits
beside. Nothing dramatic happens. That is the point. Long wordless sequences and near-empty
ink pages are designed for the exact moment a reader picks up her phone instead of sleeping —
and offers her something that works with her nervous system rather than against it.
books.marketing_promise: You will feel your body start to let go, even before the night is over.
books.marketing_angle:
Positioned at the precise reading moment: late night, phone in hand, sleep not coming.
52% silent panels and the lowest ink-fill in the stillness_press catalog mean each page is
visually restful by design. Unlike sleep-hygiene content, this series doesn't instruct —
it demonstrates, chapter by chapter, what it feels like when the body starts to slow down.

books.title: Stillness Press Slice Of Life En Us 01
Stillness Press Slice Of Life Ja Jp 01
Stillness Press Slice Of Life Jp 01
Stillness Press Slice Of Life Zh Cn 01
Stillness Press Slice Of Life Zh Tw 01
Somatic Field Notes
books.description: A gentle educational manga following a skeptical journalist who is assigned to write about somatic therapy and ends up becoming a participant. Ahjan is her guide — patient, precise, never dismissive of her skepticism. The series documents her first year of somatic practice, including the sessions that didn't work and the ones that changed her relationship with her own body.
books.marketing_promise: You will feel the intellectual pleasure of learning something genuinely new about your own body — and the surprise of caring about it.
books.marketing_angle: Entry-point manga for readers new to somatic work. The journalist-skeptic frame validates reader doubt and makes exploration feel safe rather than committed.

books.title: Hands, Shoulders, Breath
books.description:
Mei is twenty-eight and has spent a long time living from the neck up. The stomach that
tightens before she speaks, the jaw she unclenches every evening — her body has always been
talking; she just didn't know how to listen. "Hands, Shoulders, Breath" follows her through
the slow relearning: a yoga class she almost leaves, a walk where she notices her own footfall,
mornings where she places a hand on her chest before getting up. Each chapter is built around
one somatic moment — one instance of body-awareness arriving before language does. The visual
grammar does the somatic work: panels pause on hands, on the texture of a floor, on breath
visible in cold air. Readers finish feeling more at home in their own skin.
books.marketing_promise: You will remember that your body is not the enemy — it is the way through.
books.marketing_angle:
Somatic healing is one of the fastest-growing wellness search categories, but almost no manga
addresses it directly. This series makes body-awareness practice visible through iyashikei
visual grammar — panels that linger on physical sensation rather than explaining it.
The 48% silent-panel ratio creates space for the reader's own somatic experience to surface
alongside the character's. Fills a clear gap between clinical somatic therapy content and
the cozy-healing manga category.

books.title: Stillness Press Supernatural Everyday En Us 01
Stillness Press Supernatural Everyday Ja Jp 01
Stillness Press Supernatural Everyday Zh Cn 01
Stillness Press Supernatural Everyday Zh Tw 01
The Alarm Within
books.description: A teacher-student josei manga in which Ahjan, a somatic therapist, guides Mio — a school nurse who handles everyone's crises — through the process of learning to read her own body's alarm signals. Each chapter focuses on one sensation, one trigger pattern, one small shift. The art maps internal states visually, showing how the nervous system's 'alarm' softens when it is met with understanding rather than suppression.
books.marketing_promise: You will feel the precise moment when your body stops being the enemy and starts being the first place you look for information.
books.marketing_angle: The teacher-student format makes somatic concepts feel like direct mentorship. For readers who want guidance, not just insight — Ahjan's presence is calm, skilled, and never clinical.

books.title: The Forest Edge
books.description: A teacher-student seinen manga in which Ahjan walks a young environmental researcher through the concept of natural limits — using the forest edge, where trees grow thin and the terrain changes, as a sustained metaphor for healthy boundaries. The student, Sota, must learn that his limits are ecological features, not moral failures.
books.marketing_promise: You will feel the grounded, almost physical sensation of understanding that your limits are not a deficiency — they are the shape of you.
books.marketing_angle: Nature-based metaphor for boundary work — rare in therapeutic manga. Appeals to readers who find psychology language alienating but respond to biological or ecological framing.

books.title: The Held Breath
books.description: A seinen manga about a translator who works across three languages and has unconsciously learned to hold his breath under cognitive load. When a health scare flags chronic oxygen deprivation, Ahjan introduces him to a breathwork practice that becomes the lens through which he examines every relationship in his life. The breath becomes a metaphor for presence, permission, and self-trust.
books.marketing_promise: You will feel the extraordinary release of a body that has finally been given permission to exhale — and the life that opens up around that permission.
books.marketing_angle: Breath as narrative engine — a novel structural choice that makes familiar territory feel fresh. For readers drawn to quiet, contemplative manga with psychological depth.

books.title: Window Season
books.description: A josei manga about a woman in her late 30s navigating perimenopause while also processing childhood emotional neglect — two nervous system disruptions arriving together. Ahjan appears as a somatic therapist who helps her separate the physiological from the historical, giving her language and tools for both. The story is honest about the complexity and refuses easy resolution.
books.marketing_promise: You will feel the strange comfort of complexity being named — and the permission to be exactly as messy and layered as you actually are.
books.marketing_angle: Rare representation of perimenopause and developmental trauma overlapping — an underserved audience with intense loyalty. Ahjan's somatic lens makes both accessible.
